<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="60" cy="40" r="2" fill="black"/></svg>
Staccato: Short and detached (dot above/below note)

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="30" cy="40" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 30 40)"/><path d="M 34 40 L 34 15" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><ellipse cx="70" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 70 30)"/><path d="M 74 30 L 74 10" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 32 45 Q 50 55 68 35" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/></svg>

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 52 40 L 68 40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
Tenuto: Held for its full value (line above/below note)

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 54 48 L 60 40 L 66 48" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/></svg>

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 52 40 L 68 45 L 52 50" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
Treble Clef: G-clef, used for high pitches
Bass Clef: F-clef, used for low pitches
Alto Clef: C-clef centered on 3rd line
Tenor Clef: C-clef centered on 4th line
Treble and Bass staves joined by a brace

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M 50 10 L 50 50" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
Bar Line: Vertical line dividing measures

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M 45 10 L 45 50 M 55 10 L 55 50" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
Double Bar Line: Two lines indicating a new section

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M 42 10 L 42 50" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 52 10 L 52 50" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="5"/></svg>
Final Bar Line: Indicates the end of the piece
Tells beats per measure and beat value
A segment of time defined by beats

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="30" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 30 30)"/><path d="M 34 30 L 34 10" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><ellipse cx="70" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 70 30)"/><path d="M 74 30 L 74 10" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 32 35 Q 50 45 68 35" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
Tie: Connects two notes of the same pitch

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="30" cy="40" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 30 40)"/><path d="M 34 40 L 34 15" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><ellipse cx="70" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 70 30)"/><path d="M 74 30 L 74 10" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 32 45 Q 50 55 68 35" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
Slur: Connects notes of different pitches

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="75" cy="30" r="2" fill="black"/></svg>
Dot (after note): Increases note value by half

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="75" cy="30" r="2" fill="black"/><circle cx="85" cy="30" r="2" fill="black"/></svg>
Double Dot (after note): Increases note value by 3/4
Trill: Rapid alternation between two notes
Upper Mordent: Single alternation with note above
Lower (Inverted) Mordent: Single alternation with note below

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 45 10 Q 45 2 52.5 2 Q 60 2 60 10 Q 60 18 67.5 18 Q 75 18 75 10" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg>
Turn: Note above, note, note below, note

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 45 10 Q 45 18 52.5 18 Q 60 18 60 10 Q 60 2 67.5 2 Q 75 2 75 10" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg>
Inverted Turn: Note below, note, note above, note

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="35" cy="20" rx="3.5" ry="2.8" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 35 20)"/><path d="M 38 20 L 38 2" stroke="black" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M 35 25 Q 47 35 58 32" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
Appoggiatura: Leaning note (takes half the main note value)

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="35" cy="20" rx="3.5" ry="2.8" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 35 20)"/><path d="M 38 20 L 38 2" stroke="black" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M 32 12 L 44 4" stroke="black" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M 35 25 Q 47 35 58 32" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
Segno: The sign used with D.S.
Repeat Sign: Repeat the music between the dots
Play the first time through a repeat
Play the second time through a repeat
Octave below: Play an octave lower
Arpeggio: Notes of a chord played in succession

<svg width="100" height="60" viewBox="0 0 100 60"><path d="M 10 10 L 90 10 M 10 20 L 90 20 M 10 30 L 90 30 M 10 40 L 90 40 M 10 50 L 90 50" fill="none" stroke="gray" stroke-width="1"/><ellipse cx="60" cy="30" rx="5" ry="4" fill="black" transform="rotate(-15 60 30)"/><path d="M 64 30 L 64 5" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M 45 50 Q 60 35 75 50" fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="60" cy="46" r="2" fill="black"/></svg>
Fermata: Hold the note longer than indicated
Caesura: A pause or break in music
Lamb of God (often used in Requiem/Mass)
Notes that sound the same but have different names (e.g., C# and Db)
Belonging to the current scale or key
Using notes outside the current scale/key
Emphasizing the weak beats or off-beats
Shifting music to a different pitch level
Changing from one key to another
Major and minor keys that share the same key signature
Major and minor keys that share the same tonic
A continually repeated musical phrase or rhythm
Restatement of a motif at a higher or lower pitch
Turning a melody or chord upside down
A melodic or harmonic configuration that creates a sense of resolution
Ending a minor-key piece with a major chord
A short musical idea or fragment
A complete musical thought used as a building block
The relationship between independent melodic lines
A contrapuntal composition based on a main theme (subject)
A collection of short musical pieces
An introductory piece to an opera or larger work
A large-scale musical work for orchestra and voices, usually on a religious theme
A self-contained piece for a single voice, usually in an opera
A style of delivery in which a singer is allowed to adopt the rhythms of ordinary speech
The quality or 'color' of a sound
How melodic, rhythmic, and harmonic materials are combined
A single melodic line without accompaniment
A primary melody with chordal accompaniment
Multiple versions of the same melody played simultaneously
Absence of a tonal center or key
Use of two different keys simultaneously
Use of intervals smaller than a semitone
A scale consisting entirely of whole steps
A scale consisting entirely of half steps
An eight-note scale alternating whole and half steps
Sharp: Raises a note by a half step
Flat: Lowers a note by a half step
Natural: Cancels a previous sharp or flat
Double Sharp: Raises a note by a whole step
Double Flat: Lowers a note by a whole step
An interval of 2 semitones (e.g., C to D)
An interval of 1 semitone (e.g., C to Db)
An interval of 4 semitones (e.g., C to E)
An interval of 3 semitones (e.g., C to Eb)
An interval of 5 semitones (e.g., C to F)
An interval of 7 semitones (e.g., C to G)
An interval of 9 semitones (e.g., C to A)
An interval of 8 semitones (e.g., C to Ab)
An interval of 11 semitones (e.g., C to B)
An interval of 10 semitones (e.g., C to Bb)
An interval of 12 semitones (e.g., C to C)
